stories.title: Trump weighs doubling refugee cap for white South Africans
stories.description: The administration is discussing adding 10,000 spots to the current 7,500 ceiling, nearly all earmarked for Afrikaners, according to Reuters.

stories.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K on Polymarket
stories.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly used knowledge of the Maduro capture operation to place bets on prediction markets, prosecutors say.

stories.title: FDA clears first gene therapy for inherited hearing loss
stories.description: Regeneron says it will provide Otarmeni, which restored hearing in children born deaf, for free to eligible U.S. patients.

stories.title: Trump taps personal pool contractor to renovate Reflecting Pool
stories.description: The president said his hotel contractor could complete the work in about a week for $1.5 million, bypassing a $301 million government bid.

stories.title: Iran war strains US ability to arm and defend Taiwan
stories.description: A naval blockade, depleted munitions, and a stalled $14 billion arms deal are stretching Pentagon resources as China watches closely.

stories.title: DOJ targets 384 citizens for denaturalization in 'first wave'
stories.description: Prosecutors in 39 regional offices will pursue citizenship revocation cases, a sharp escalation from the historical average of 11 cases per year.

stories.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended three weeks
stories.description: The announcement came after Israeli and Lebanese diplomats held a second round of rare direct talks in Washington, days before the truce was set to expire.

stories.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs starting May 20, more layoffs planned for 2026
stories.description: The first wave targets about 10% of Meta's global workforce as the company pivots toward AI and away from divisions like Reality Labs.

stories.title: Wall Street slips from record highs as oil tops $103
stories.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq pulled back Thursday as Strait of Hormuz disruptions pushed Brent crude higher and U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled.

stories.title: DOJ inspector general opens audit of Epstein files compliance
stories.description: The review will examine how the department identified, redacted, and released records under a law it has been accused of violating.

stories.title: U.S. seizes another Iranian tanker as Hormuz crisis deepens
stories.description: The Pentagon captured an oil tanker carrying Iranian crude in the Indian Ocean on Thursday, escalating a tit-for-tat standoff after Iran seized two merchant ships.

stories.title: 40,000 Samsung workers stage largest protest, threaten strike
stories.description: Workers rallied at Samsung's chip complex in Pyeongtaek, demanding higher bonuses and threatening an 18-day walkout starting May 21.

stories.title: JetBlue sued over alleged use of personal data to set fares
stories.description: A class action filed in Brooklyn federal court claims the airline uses tracking technology and customer data to inflate ticket prices.

stories.title: Microsoft offers first voluntary buyout in its 51-year history
stories.description: About 7% of U.S. employees are eligible for the retirement packages as the company continues reshaping its workforce around AI.

stories.title: France probes weather sensor tampering tied to Polymarket bets
stories.description: Météo-France filed a criminal complaint after suspicious temperature spikes at a Paris airport sensor aligned with $34,000 in prediction market wins.

stories.title: California's wildfire deforestation rivals global hotspots
stories.description: California lost conifer forest at up to three times the global average rate from 2001 to 2023, with reforestation efforts barely keeping pace, according to researchers.

stories.title: China warns of 'critical juncture' as Iran ceasefire talks falter
stories.description: Beijing urged all parties to prevent a return to war after scheduled U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan stalled and Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining a naval blockade.

stories.title: EU formally approves €90B Ukraine loan, new Russia sanctions
stories.description: Leaders gathering in Cyprus for a two-day summit cleared the long-stalled aid package and a 20th round of sanctions targeting Moscow's energy and financial sectors.

stories.title: Oklo and NVIDIA team up with Los Alamos on nuclear fuel for AI factories
stories.description: The partnership combines Oklo's reactor technology, NVIDIA's AI infrastructure, and the lab's materials science expertise to support the DOE's Genesis Mission.

stories.title: DOJ reclassifies medical marijuana to Schedule III
stories.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche signed the order Thursday, delivering on Trump's December executive order and marking the biggest federal marijuana policy shift since 1970.

stories.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS traced to cold, ancient corner of the Milky Way
stories.description: Scientists say the third confirmed interstellar visitor likely formed up to 11 billion years ago in a frigid, isolated region before its host star's planetary system took shape.

stories.title: Senate adopts GOP budget plan to fund ICE, Border Patrol
stories.description: The 50-48 vote early Thursday advances a partisan $70 billion plan to reopen DHS, bypassing Democrats through budget reconciliation.

stories.title: Trump says China helped push Iran toward ceasefire talks
stories.description: Beijing used its leverage as Iran's top oil buyer to urge Tehran back to the negotiating table, diplomats told the Associated Press.

stories.title: SpaceX reveals in-house GPU plans in pre-IPO filing
stories.description: The company flagged chip supply risks and "substantial capital expenditures" for AI hardware as it prepares for a potential record-setting public offering.

stories.title: Tesla raises capex plan to $25 billion for AI push
stories.description: The EV maker now expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 as it triples spending on robots, autonomy and AI infrastructure.

stories.title: Nuclear leaders convene in Busan, framing reactors as AI-era infrastructure
stories.description: Some 19,000 participants from 19 countries gathered in South Korea this week to discuss nuclear power's role in meeting surging AI electricity demand.

stories.title: Asian shares retreat from record highs as oil tops $103 on Iran tensions
stories.description: Japan's Nikkei crossed 60,000 for the first time and South Korea's Kospi hit a record before profit-taking reversed gains across the region.

stories.title: RFK Jr. tells Senate his department advises all children get measles vaccine
stories.description: The former anti-vaccine activist offered a firm defense of the MMR shot amid nearly 1,750 confirmed U.S. measles cases this year.

stories.title: IDF removes 40 Israeli settlers who crossed into Syria
stories.description: The far-right Pioneers of Bashan group barricaded themselves on a rooftop in a Syrian village, demanding approval for Jewish settlements.

stories.title: White House says Iran ship seizures don't breach ceasefire
stories.description: Iran's IRGC seized two commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, but the White House insisted the incident does not violate the extended U.S.-Iran truce.

stories.title: Study links 988 hotline to 4,400 fewer youth suicides
stories.description: Harvard researchers found suicide deaths among young Americans fell 11% below projections in the first two and a half years after the crisis line launched.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills journalist in southern Lebanon
stories.description: Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam called the killing of Al-Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil a "blatant war crime" and vowed to pursue legal action.

stories.title: House panel split over pardoning Maxwell for Epstein testimony
stories.description: Chairman Comer said some Republicans favor a clemency deal, but he opposes it, calling Maxwell "the worst person" after Epstein.

stories.title: Senate defeats fifth bid to curb Trump's war powers on Iran
stories.description: Democrats vowed weekly votes as the conflict's 60-day War Powers Act deadline nears May 1, raising pressure on Republicans to act.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills 5 near mosque in northern Gaza
stories.description: Three children were among those killed in Beit Lahia, Gaza's civil defense said, as violence persists despite the October ceasefire.

stories.title: Virginia judge blocks certification of redistricting vote
stories.description: A circuit court ruled the voter-approved referendum unconstitutional. Attorney General Jay Jones says the state will appeal.

stories.title: Iran denies Trump's claim he stopped execution of eight women
stories.description: Trump said Iran halted plans to execute eight female protesters at his request, but Tehran's judiciary called the assertion "fake news."

stories.title: UK to pay France £660M in new deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is set to sign a three-year agreement on Thursday that includes a riot squad and expanded patrols in northern France.

stories.title: FBI investigated NYT reporter who covered Patel's girlfriend
stories.description: The probe into reporter Elizabeth Williamson began after she detailed how FBI Director Kash Patel used bureau personnel to escort his girlfriend to events and errands.

stories.title: Chemical leak at West Virginia plant kills 2, sends 19 to hospital
stories.description: A violent reaction between nitric acid and another substance released hydrogen sulfide gas at a silver recovery facility in Institute, W.Va.

stories.title: White House denies firm deadline for Iran ceasefire extension
stories.description: Press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday that Trump has set no timetable for Iran to respond, rejecting reports of a three-to-five-day window.

stories.title: House pulls Endangered Species Act overhaul after bipartisan outcry on Earth Day
stories.description: Speaker Johnson withdrew H.R. 1897 from the House floor Wednesday after the bill lacked enough Republican votes to pass.

stories.title: Zelensky distances himself from 'Donnyland' Donbas proposal, pushes to restart talks
stories.description: Ukraine's president insisted he uses only official names for Donetsk and Luhansk, while calling for resumed U.S.-mediated peace negotiations.

stories.title: RFK Jr. tells senators new CDC director will be free to make her own decisions
stories.description: The pledge came after Kennedy refused earlier this week to commit to backing nominee Erica Schwartz's vaccine guidance without interference.

stories.title: 9th Circuit blocks California law requiring ICE agents to show ID
stories.description: The appeals court ruled the No Vigilantes Act unconstitutionally regulates the federal government, reversing a lower court decision that had upheld the law.

stories.title: Google commits $750M to agentic AI, unveils new chips
stories.description: The announcements at Cloud Next 2026 include the largest partner fund from any major cloud provider and Google's first split training-inference TPUs.

stories.title: Georgia Rep. David Scott dies at 80 after 50 years in office
stories.description: Scott, a 12-term Democrat representing the Atlanta area, becomes the fifth House member to die during the 119th Congress.

stories.title: NASA marks Earth Day with Artemis II lunar photos
stories.description: The agency released posters featuring the crew's "Earthset" image, captured as Orion passed behind the Moon's far side on April 6.

stories.title: Trump administration nears $500M rescue loan for Spirit Airlines
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reports the deal would provide emergency funding to the discount carrier, which faced possible liquidation just days ago.

stories.title: US deploys Ukrainian counter-drone system at Saudi base
stories.description: Sky Map, a battle-tested Ukrainian command-and-control platform, is now operational at Prince Sultan Air Base to counter Iranian drone attacks.

stories.title: JWST detects water ice clouds on Jupiter-like exoplanet
stories.description: Thick, patchy clouds resembling Earth's cirrus were found on Epsilon Indi Ab, just 12 light-years away, challenging standard exoplanet atmosphere models.

stories.title: $430M oil bet placed minutes before Trump's Iran ceasefire extension
stories.description: Traders wagered $430 million on falling crude prices just 15 minutes before the announcement, the fourth such suspiciously timed trade this year.

stories.title: OpenAI plans $1.5B investment in PE joint venture
stories.description: The AI firm would embed its tools across portfolio companies through a $10 billion venture dubbed "DeployCo," the Financial Times reported.

stories.title: US burned through half its missile stocks in Iran war, analysis finds
stories.description: A new report warns rebuilding depleted inventories will take years, leaving the U.S. vulnerable in the Western Pacific against China.

stories.title: Trump claims Iran 'collapsing financially' amid US naval blockade
stories.description: As a fragile ceasefire holds, Trump said Tehran is losing $500 million daily and its security forces are going unpaid.

stories.title: Appeals court keeps 'Alligator Alcatraz' open in Everglades
stories.description: The Eleventh Circuit ruled the Florida-operated facility isn't subject to federal environmental review, overturning a judge's order to wind down operations.

stories.title: CATL unveils EV battery with 621-mile range, sub-7-minute charge
stories.description: The world's largest EV battery maker launched six innovations at its Super Technology Day in Beijing, widening its lead over rivals.

stories.title: Trump's White House ballroom contract reveals anonymous donor protections
stories.description: The agreement, released after a court-ordered FOIA disclosure, exempts the White House from conflict of interest reviews governing the $400 million project.

stories.title: Fifth Circuit upholds Texas Ten Commandments classroom law
stories.description: The 9-8 en banc ruling reverses a lower court injunction, and civil rights groups say they will ask the Supreme Court to intervene.

stories.title: Duffy marks one year of air traffic control overhaul, seeks $20B more from Congress
stories.description: The Transportation secretary said the modernization is mostly on track but told lawmakers the FAA needs billions beyond the $12.5 billion already approved.

stories.title: Trump's economic approval drops to 30% as Iran war fuels inflation
stories.description: An AP-NORC poll found only 33% of adults approve of his overall job performance, with Republican support also declining sharply.

stories.title: Americans killed in Mexico crash were CIA officers
stories.description: The revelation has deepened a diplomatic rift, with President Sheinbaum ordering a probe into whether the operation violated Mexico's sovereignty laws.

stories.title: Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick resigns ahead of expulsion vote
stories.description: The Florida Democrat quit Congress Tuesday just before an Ethics Committee hearing expected to recommend her removal over alleged theft of $5 million in pandemic relief funds.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump permitting rules targeting wind and solar projects
stories.description: A federal judge in Boston issued a preliminary injunction after finding the administration singled out renewable developers without clear rationale.

stories.title: Macron pledges support for Lebanon ahead of Israel talks
stories.description: France's president met Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam in Paris to bolster Beirut's position before Thursday's U.S.-hosted negotiations with Israel.

stories.title: NATO revives Cold War-era sub hunting as Russia pushes into Arctic
stories.description: Bloomberg reports that Russia's upgraded nuclear submarines are forcing NATO to overhaul anti-submarine warfare across the Arctic and North Atlantic.

stories.title: Warsh assures senators Trump won’t dictate Fed policy on rates
stories.description: Trump's Fed chair nominee testified he was never asked to commit to rate cuts, but a GOP senator's hold over the Powell probe clouds his path.

stories.title: Dutch intelligence warns Chinese cyberattacks on West will intensify
stories.description: The MIVD's 2025 annual report called on Europe to take greater responsibility for its own security as the rules-based international order erodes.

stories.title: UNESCO report finds its sites sheltering 60% of mapped species
stories.description: The first comprehensive assessment of all 2,260-plus UNESCO-designated sites found wildlife populations there held steady despite a 73% global decline.

stories.title: FBI, House Oversight launch parallel probes into missing and dead scientists
stories.description: At least 11 researchers linked to nuclear, aerospace, and defense work have died or vanished since 2022, prompting dual federal and congressional investigations.

stories.title: Trump praises 'very good talks' with Anthropic after months-long feud
stories.description: The president's remarks Tuesday mark a sharp reversal from ordering agencies to cut ties with the AI company in February.

stories.title: Feds cut water releases amid historic Western drought
stories.description: Colorado River system reservoirs hold just 36% of capacity as record-low snowpack and extreme heat compound the crisis.

stories.title: Curiosity rover detects 'origin-of-life' molecules on Mars
stories.description: A first-of-its-kind chemical experiment on the Red Planet found over 20 organic compounds, including a possible DNA precursor never before seen on Mars.

stories.title: Trump's Board of Peace in talks with DP World on Gaza rebuild
stories.description: A draft proposal envisions a "port-centric economic ecosystem" and supply chain system, as part of US efforts to privatise Gaza's reconstruction.

stories.title: Japan scraps postwar ban on lethal weapons exports
stories.description: Prime Minister Takaichi's Cabinet approved the sale of fighter jets, missiles, and warships to 17 partner nations.

stories.title: Bezos's AI lab nears $10B round at $38B valuation
stories.description: Project Prometheus, focused on physical AI for manufacturing and engineering, counts JPMorgan and BlackRock among its backers, according to the Financial Times.

stories.title: Trump, GOP stockpile $1 billion for midterms amid shifting polls
stories.description: New filings show Republicans hold roughly $1 billion in campaign cash, but the Cook Political Report has shifted four Senate races toward Democrats.

stories.title: Over 4,200 US flights delayed as aviation crisis hits day 20
stories.description: Severe spring weather drove 4,231 delays and 79 cancellations Monday, extending a post-Easter disruption streak with no recovery in sight.

stories.title: Senate Democrats demand Hegseth explain civilian deaths in Iran
stories.description: Eleven senators set a May 4 deadline for the defense secretary to justify cuts to Pentagon programs designed to protect noncombatants.

stories.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to fund energy projects amid Iran war
stories.description: Five presidential determinations target coal, LNG, petroleum and grid infrastructure as gas prices hover near $4 a gallon.

stories.title: Iraq, Syria reopen Rabia border crossing after 13 years
stories.description: The reopening restores all official border crossings between the two countries for the first time since civil war and ISIS disrupted the region.

stories.title: Apple names John Ternus CEO as Tim Cook shifts to chairman role
stories.description: Cook, who led Apple since 2011, will become executive chairman on Sept. 1 after a unanimous board vote.

stories.title: Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer resigns amid misconduct probe
stories.description: She is the third Cabinet secretary to leave Trump's second term, following months of inspector general scrutiny over alleged personal misconduct.

stories.title: Vaccine-hesitant parents shift course as measles surges across the Americas
stories.description: PAHO reports over 15,000 confirmed cases in the first 14 weeks of 2026, with two-thirds of patients unvaccinated.

stories.title: Singer D4vd charged with first-degree murder of teen found in his Tesla
stories.description: LA County DA Nathan Hochman announced charges Monday against the 21-year-old artist in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

stories.title: Starmer admits misleading Parliament over Mandelson vetting scandal
stories.description: The prime minister told MPs the error was inadvertent, but all major opposition parties have called for his resignation.

stories.title: FBI Director Patel sues The Atlantic for $250M over report on drinking
stories.description: The defamation lawsuit, filed Monday in D.C. federal court, alleges the magazine published false claims about his conduct with "actual malice."

stories.title: UK pushes for toll-free Hormuz as ceasefire nears expiry
stories.description: Cooper warns that the wrong precedent on navigation freedom in the strait would be "deeply damaging" for global economy and security.

stories.title: CBP launches portal to refund $166B in tariffs struck down by Supreme Court
stories.description: The CAPE system went live Monday at 8 a.m. ET, letting importers file claims in what could be the largest tariff repayment in U.S. history.

stories.title: Supreme Court to hear Catholic preschools' challenge to Colorado LGBTQ nondiscrimination rules
stories.description: The case tests whether religious schools can receive public funding while declining to enroll children from same-sex families.

stories.title: Expert says Trump's Iran nuclear claim is 'just not true'
stories.description: Harvard nuclear adviser told 60 Minutes that Iran still has enough enriched uranium for 10 to 11 bombs despite U.S. and Israeli strikes.

stories.title: UAE warns it may settle oil trades in yuan without US dollar backstop
stories.description: Abu Dhabi told Washington the Iran war has strained its reserves, and without a Fed swap line, it could pivot energy sales to Chinese currency.

stories.title: Eni announces giant gas discovery offshore Indonesia
stories.description: The Geliga-1 well in the Kutei Basin holds an estimated 5 trillion cubic feet of gas and 300 million barrels of condensate.

stories.title: Samsung SDI signs first EV battery deal with Mercedes-Benz
stories.description: The multi-year agreement will supply high-nickel batteries for Mercedes-Benz's next-generation compact and mid-size electric SUVs and coupes.

stories.title: UniCredit unveils plan to transform Commerzbank
stories.description: CEO Andrea Orcel is presenting a "value creation" strategy for the German lender, arguing it is too focused on short-term results.

stories.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3, raising toll to 181
stories.description: Sunday's Caribbean strike is the latest in Operation Southern Spear, a monthslong campaign that legal experts say violates international law.

stories.title: Hegseth berated Army secretary in early clash, WSJ reports
stories.description: A Wall Street Journal report reveals the defense secretary shouted at Dan Driscoll to "stay in his lane" soon after Driscoll's first day at the Pentagon.

stories.title: Avalanche top Kings 2-1 in Stanley Cup playoff opener
stories.description: Scott Wedgewood stopped 24 shots in his playoff debut as Colorado took Game 1 of the Western Conference First Round on Sunday.

stories.title: Over 200 immigration judges replaced with 'deportation judges'
stories.description: Former judges tell CBS News the unprecedented overhaul dismantles due process, as asylum approval rates fall to historic lows.

stories.title: Israel building permanent military bases in Gaza, satellite images show
stories.description: An Al Jazeera investigation found Israeli forces are expanding fortified outposts across Gaza as civilian reconstruction stalls and ceasefire violations mount.

stories.title: FBI's Patel vows imminent arrests over 2020 election
stories.description: Michigan officials issued a joint statement Sunday rejecting DOJ demands for 2024 ballots, calling the effort election interference.

stories.title: Blue Origin reuses New Glenn booster but puts satellite in wrong orbit
stories.description: The NG-3 mission marked New Glenn's first booster reuse, but an upper stage issue left AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite off course.

stories.title: Hamas signals readiness to hand over some weapons in Gaza
stories.description: Two officials told The New York Times the group would surrender police and security weapons, but its military wing's arsenal remains untouched.

stories.title: Syrian billionaires invoked Trump name to help kill Caesar Act sanctions, NYT reports
stories.description: The Al-Khayyat family lobbied Washington while securing over $12 billion in Syrian reconstruction contracts, according to the Times.

stories.title: 8 children killed in mass shooting in Shreveport, La.
stories.description: Police say 10 people were shot in a domestic disturbance that spanned three locations; victims ranged in age from 1 to 14.

stories.title: Iran regains 60% of missile launchers during ceasefire, US intelligence estimates
stories.description: Tehran has excavated about 100 additional launchers from bombed sites since the April 8 ceasefire, according to a New York Times report.

stories.title: FBI, DOJ rush to rebuild after losing thousands of staff
stories.description: The agencies are relaxing hiring standards and fast-tracking recruitment after mass departures during the Trump administration, according to the AP.

stories.title: Vance strikes conciliatory tone with Pope Leo XIV after week of White House-Vatican tensions
stories.description: The vice president wrote on X that he was "grateful" to the pope, days after warning him to "be careful" on theology.

stories.title: Iran calls free transit through Hormuz 'a fiction' as Europe plans naval mission
stories.description: Tehran asserted its right to control the strait, a day after 40-plus nations met in Paris to plan a multinational force to restore shipping access.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed as Lebanon ceasefire frays
stories.description: A French UN soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday as Israel continued shelling despite Trump declaring strikes "prohibited."

stories.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile into East Sea
stories.description: South Korea's JCS detected at least one missile launched eastward on Sunday, the sixth known ballistic missile test by Pyongyang this year.

stories.title: Penn State develops 3D-printed brain sensors tailored to each patient
stories.description: Hydrogel electrodes shaped from MRI scans matched individual brain folds, improving signal quality without immune response in rat tests.

stories.title: Trump Organization plans 70-story tower in Tbilisi, Georgia
stories.description: The branded skyscraper would be the tallest building in the Georgian capital, marking the family firm's latest foreign deal during Trump's second term.

stories.title: NATO chief Rutte calls US withdrawal speculation 'absurd'
stories.description: Rutte told a German newspaper the American nuclear umbrella will remain Europe's "ultimate guarantor of security."

stories.title: Iranians fear regime will tighten grip once ceasefire holds
stories.description: As a fragile truce pauses U.S.-Israeli strikes, Iranians told Reuters they dread the regime redirecting its full coercive apparatus inward.

stories.title: Trump backs 7 challengers against Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
stories.description: Indiana's May 5 primary tests whether Trump can punish state lawmakers who defied his push to redraw congressional maps.

stories.title: Egypt, Pakistan building framework for lasting US-Iran peace
stories.description: Four regional powers are intensifying diplomacy at the Antalya forum as a fragile ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

stories.title: Australia, Japan sign $7B stealth frigate deal
stories.description: The A$10 billion contract for 11 Mogami-class warships is Japan's biggest defense export since lifting its postwar military export ban.

stories.title: Trump signs bill extending spy powers until April 30
stories.description: The 10-day stopgap followed a chaotic overnight House session where conservative Republicans blocked longer renewals of warrantless surveillance authority.

stories.title: Tornado outbreak batters Midwest as severe storms continue
stories.description: At least 10 tornadoes were reported Friday as storms unleashed massive hail and destructive winds from Texas to the Great Lakes.

stories.title: Israeli fire kills two UNICEF water truck drivers in Gaza
stories.description: UNICEF suspended operations at northern Gaza's only truck filling point for the water supply line serving Gaza City after the attack.

stories.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani meets with Obama amid feud with Trump
stories.description: The sit-down at a Bronx child-care center caps months of rapport-building between the democratic socialist mayor and the former president.

stories.title: Alito not expected to retire, sources say, cooling Supreme Court vacancy talk
stories.description: The 76-year-old justice has hired clerks for next term, ending weeks of speculation about a Trump appointment opportunity.

stories.title: Pakistan deployed fighter jets to escort Iran delegation home from talks
stories.description: About two dozen jets escorted Iranian negotiators to Tehran after officials raised concerns about a possible Israeli strike on their aircraft.

stories.title: Trump reverses course, renews Russian oil sanctions waiver
stories.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent said just two days earlier that the waiver would not be renewed. The new license runs through May 16.

stories.title: UNICEF reports 172 children killed as Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
stories.description: The U.S.-brokered 10-day truce began at midnight local time on April 17 after 46 days of fighting that killed over 2,100 people.

stories.title: RFK Jr. calls Danish Tylenol-autism study 'garbage' at House hearing
stories.description: Kennedy dismissed research on 1.5 million children finding no link between prenatal acetaminophen use and autism, demanding its retraction.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track psychedelic drug reviews
stories.description: The executive order directs the FDA to expedite approval of MDMA, psilocybin, and other psychedelics and allocates $50 million for ibogaine research.

stories.title: Breeding top 1-5% of corals could boost reef survival, study finds
stories.description: Newcastle University-led research published in Current Biology shows assisted evolution can help corals endure heatwaves, but only with aggressive, multigenerational selection.

stories.title: Xanadu CEO becomes billionaire as Nvidia quantum AI push lifts stock
stories.description: Christian Weedbrook's 51.8% stake in the photonic quantum computing firm is now worth over $1.6 billion, three weeks after its IPO.

stories.title: Appeals court lets Trump resume White House ballroom work
stories.description: A three-judge panel temporarily lifted a lower court injunction, allowing the $400 million project to continue ahead of a June 5 hearing.

stories.title: FAA receives 6,000 air traffic controller applications in 12 hours
stories.description: The gamer-targeted recruitment campaign drew a flood of interest as the agency, roughly 3,000 controllers short, nears its 8,000-application cap.

stories.title: Data center industry scrambles as backlash threatens trillions in projects
stories.description: Industry executives worry a coordinated response is lacking as communities block projects and states reconsider billions in tax breaks.

stories.title: Israeli drone kills one in southern Lebanon hours into ceasefire
stories.description: Trump declared Israel "PROHIBITED" from bombing Lebanon, but an Israeli drone struck a motorcyclist in the south on the first day of the truce.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV draws massive crowd at Douala Mass
stories.description: The pope challenged corruption and urged youth to reject violence during the largest gathering of his 11-day African tour.

stories.title: Researchers produce hydrogen from alcohol using iron and UV light
stories.description: A control experiment that was expected to fail led to a simple method rivaling the performance of expensive catalysts.

stories.title: Iran's buried Pickaxe Mountain site looms over nuclear talks
stories.description: With an April 22 ceasefire deadline days away, analysts are pressing Trump to neutralize a deeply buried facility that U.S. bombs cannot reach.

stories.title: Newsom's PAC spent $1.5M buying his memoir for donors
stories.description: New federal filings show about 67,000 supporters received copies, accounting for roughly two-thirds of the book's total print sales.

stories.title: Wright tells Congress AI data centers are straining the grid
stories.description: The Energy secretary warned that surging electricity demand from AI and reshoring is putting historic pressure on U.S. power infrastructure.

stories.title: Trump seeks 90-day pause in $10B IRS suit amid talks
stories.description: Lawyers for the president filed a motion Friday to halt the case while both sides negotiate a potential settlement with the Justice Department's consent.

top.title: Trump weighs doubling refugee cap for white South Africans
top.description: The administration is discussing adding 10,000 spots to the current 7,500 ceiling, nearly all earmarked for Afrikaners, according to Reuters.

top.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K on Polymarket
top.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly used knowledge of the Maduro capture operation to place bets on prediction markets, prosecutors say.

top.title: FDA clears first gene therapy for inherited hearing loss
top.description: Regeneron says it will provide Otarmeni, which restored hearing in children born deaf, for free to eligible U.S. patients.

top.title: Trump taps personal pool contractor to renovate Reflecting Pool
top.description: The president said his hotel contractor could complete the work in about a week for $1.5 million, bypassing a $301 million government bid.

top.title: Iran war strains US ability to arm and defend Taiwan
top.description: A naval blockade, depleted munitions, and a stalled $14 billion arms deal are stretching Pentagon resources as China watches closely.

top.title: DOJ targets 384 citizens for denaturalization in 'first wave'
top.description: Prosecutors in 39 regional offices will pursue citizenship revocation cases, a sharp escalation from the historical average of 11 cases per year.

top.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended three weeks
top.description: The announcement came after Israeli and Lebanese diplomats held a second round of rare direct talks in Washington, days before the truce was set to expire.

top.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs starting May 20, more layoffs planned for 2026
top.description: The first wave targets about 10% of Meta's global workforce as the company pivots toward AI and away from divisions like Reality Labs.

top.title: Wall Street slips from record highs as oil tops $103
top.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq pulled back Thursday as Strait of Hormuz disruptions pushed Brent crude higher and U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled.

top.title: DOJ inspector general opens audit of Epstein files compliance
top.description: The review will examine how the department identified, redacted, and released records under a law it has been accused of violating.

top.title: U.S. seizes another Iranian tanker as Hormuz crisis deepens
top.description: The Pentagon captured an oil tanker carrying Iranian crude in the Indian Ocean on Thursday, escalating a tit-for-tat standoff after Iran seized two merchant ships.

top.title: 40,000 Samsung workers stage largest protest, threaten strike
top.description: Workers rallied at Samsung's chip complex in Pyeongtaek, demanding higher bonuses and threatening an 18-day walkout starting May 21.

top.title: JetBlue sued over alleged use of personal data to set fares
top.description: A class action filed in Brooklyn federal court claims the airline uses tracking technology and customer data to inflate ticket prices.

top.title: Microsoft offers first voluntary buyout in its 51-year history
top.description: About 7% of U.S. employees are eligible for the retirement packages as the company continues reshaping its workforce around AI.

top.title: France probes weather sensor tampering tied to Polymarket bets
top.description: Météo-France filed a criminal complaint after suspicious temperature spikes at a Paris airport sensor aligned with $34,000 in prediction market wins.

top.title: California's wildfire deforestation rivals global hotspots
top.description: California lost conifer forest at up to three times the global average rate from 2001 to 2023, with reforestation efforts barely keeping pace, according to researchers.

top.title: China warns of 'critical juncture' as Iran ceasefire talks falter
top.description: Beijing urged all parties to prevent a return to war after scheduled U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan stalled and Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining a naval blockade.

top.title: EU formally approves €90B Ukraine loan, new Russia sanctions
top.description: Leaders gathering in Cyprus for a two-day summit cleared the long-stalled aid package and a 20th round of sanctions targeting Moscow's energy and financial sectors.

top.title: Oklo and NVIDIA team up with Los Alamos on nuclear fuel for AI factories
top.description: The partnership combines Oklo's reactor technology, NVIDIA's AI infrastructure, and the lab's materials science expertise to support the DOE's Genesis Mission.

top.title: DOJ reclassifies medical marijuana to Schedule III
top.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche signed the order Thursday, delivering on Trump's December executive order and marking the biggest federal marijuana policy shift since 1970.

top.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS traced to cold, ancient corner of the Milky Way
top.description: Scientists say the third confirmed interstellar visitor likely formed up to 11 billion years ago in a frigid, isolated region before its host star's planetary system took shape.

top.title: Senate adopts GOP budget plan to fund ICE, Border Patrol
top.description: The 50-48 vote early Thursday advances a partisan $70 billion plan to reopen DHS, bypassing Democrats through budget reconciliation.

top.title: Trump says China helped push Iran toward ceasefire talks
top.description: Beijing used its leverage as Iran's top oil buyer to urge Tehran back to the negotiating table, diplomats told the Associated Press.

top.title: SpaceX reveals in-house GPU plans in pre-IPO filing
top.description: The company flagged chip supply risks and "substantial capital expenditures" for AI hardware as it prepares for a potential record-setting public offering.

top.title: Tesla raises capex plan to $25 billion for AI push
top.description: The EV maker now expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 as it triples spending on robots, autonomy and AI infrastructure.

top.title: Nuclear leaders convene in Busan, framing reactors as AI-era infrastructure
top.description: Some 19,000 participants from 19 countries gathered in South Korea this week to discuss nuclear power's role in meeting surging AI electricity demand.

top.title: Asian shares retreat from record highs as oil tops $103 on Iran tensions
top.description: Japan's Nikkei crossed 60,000 for the first time and South Korea's Kospi hit a record before profit-taking reversed gains across the region.

top.title: RFK Jr. tells Senate his department advises all children get measles vaccine
top.description: The former anti-vaccine activist offered a firm defense of the MMR shot amid nearly 1,750 confirmed U.S. measles cases this year.

top.title: IDF removes 40 Israeli settlers who crossed into Syria
top.description: The far-right Pioneers of Bashan group barricaded themselves on a rooftop in a Syrian village, demanding approval for Jewish settlements.

top.title: White House says Iran ship seizures don't breach ceasefire
top.description: Iran's IRGC seized two commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, but the White House insisted the incident does not violate the extended U.S.-Iran truce.

top.title: Study links 988 hotline to 4,400 fewer youth suicides
top.description: Harvard researchers found suicide deaths among young Americans fell 11% below projections in the first two and a half years after the crisis line launched.

top.title: Israeli airstrike kills journalist in southern Lebanon
top.description: Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam called the killing of Al-Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil a "blatant war crime" and vowed to pursue legal action.

top.title: House panel split over pardoning Maxwell for Epstein testimony
top.description: Chairman Comer said some Republicans favor a clemency deal, but he opposes it, calling Maxwell "the worst person" after Epstein.

top.title: Senate defeats fifth bid to curb Trump's war powers on Iran
top.description: Democrats vowed weekly votes as the conflict's 60-day War Powers Act deadline nears May 1, raising pressure on Republicans to act.

top.title: Israeli airstrike kills 5 near mosque in northern Gaza
top.description: Three children were among those killed in Beit Lahia, Gaza's civil defense said, as violence persists despite the October ceasefire.

top.title: Virginia judge blocks certification of redistricting vote
top.description: A circuit court ruled the voter-approved referendum unconstitutional. Attorney General Jay Jones says the state will appeal.

top.title: Iran denies Trump's claim he stopped execution of eight women
top.description: Trump said Iran halted plans to execute eight female protesters at his request, but Tehran's judiciary called the assertion "fake news."

top.title: UK to pay France £660M in new deal to curb Channel crossings
top.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is set to sign a three-year agreement on Thursday that includes a riot squad and expanded patrols in northern France.

top.title: FBI investigated NYT reporter who covered Patel's girlfriend
top.description: The probe into reporter Elizabeth Williamson began after she detailed how FBI Director Kash Patel used bureau personnel to escort his girlfriend to events and errands.

top.title: Chemical leak at West Virginia plant kills 2, sends 19 to hospital
top.description: A violent reaction between nitric acid and another substance released hydrogen sulfide gas at a silver recovery facility in Institute, W.Va.

top.title: White House denies firm deadline for Iran ceasefire extension
top.description: Press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday that Trump has set no timetable for Iran to respond, rejecting reports of a three-to-five-day window.

top.title: House pulls Endangered Species Act overhaul after bipartisan outcry on Earth Day
top.description: Speaker Johnson withdrew H.R. 1897 from the House floor Wednesday after the bill lacked enough Republican votes to pass.

top.title: Zelensky distances himself from 'Donnyland' Donbas proposal, pushes to restart talks
top.description: Ukraine's president insisted he uses only official names for Donetsk and Luhansk, while calling for resumed U.S.-mediated peace negotiations.

top.title: RFK Jr. tells senators new CDC director will be free to make her own decisions
top.description: The pledge came after Kennedy refused earlier this week to commit to backing nominee Erica Schwartz's vaccine guidance without interference.

top.title: 9th Circuit blocks California law requiring ICE agents to show ID
top.description: The appeals court ruled the No Vigilantes Act unconstitutionally regulates the federal government, reversing a lower court decision that had upheld the law.

top.title: Google commits $750M to agentic AI, unveils new chips
top.description: The announcements at Cloud Next 2026 include the largest partner fund from any major cloud provider and Google's first split training-inference TPUs.

top.title: Georgia Rep. David Scott dies at 80 after 50 years in office
top.description: Scott, a 12-term Democrat representing the Atlanta area, becomes the fifth House member to die during the 119th Congress.

top.title: NASA marks Earth Day with Artemis II lunar photos
top.description: The agency released posters featuring the crew's "Earthset" image, captured as Orion passed behind the Moon's far side on April 6.

top.title: Trump administration nears $500M rescue loan for Spirit Airlines
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reports the deal would provide emergency funding to the discount carrier, which faced possible liquidation just days ago.

top.title: US deploys Ukrainian counter-drone system at Saudi base
top.description: Sky Map, a battle-tested Ukrainian command-and-control platform, is now operational at Prince Sultan Air Base to counter Iranian drone attacks.

top.title: JWST detects water ice clouds on Jupiter-like exoplanet
top.description: Thick, patchy clouds resembling Earth's cirrus were found on Epsilon Indi Ab, just 12 light-years away, challenging standard exoplanet atmosphere models.

top.title: $430M oil bet placed minutes before Trump's Iran ceasefire extension
top.description: Traders wagered $430 million on falling crude prices just 15 minutes before the announcement, the fourth such suspiciously timed trade this year.

top.title: OpenAI plans $1.5B investment in PE joint venture
top.description: The AI firm would embed its tools across portfolio companies through a $10 billion venture dubbed "DeployCo," the Financial Times reported.

top.title: US burned through half its missile stocks in Iran war, analysis finds
top.description: A new report warns rebuilding depleted inventories will take years, leaving the U.S. vulnerable in the Western Pacific against China.

top.title: Trump claims Iran 'collapsing financially' amid US naval blockade
top.description: As a fragile ceasefire holds, Trump said Tehran is losing $500 million daily and its security forces are going unpaid.

top.title: Appeals court keeps 'Alligator Alcatraz' open in Everglades
top.description: The Eleventh Circuit ruled the Florida-operated facility isn't subject to federal environmental review, overturning a judge's order to wind down operations.

top.title: CATL unveils EV battery with 621-mile range, sub-7-minute charge
top.description: The world's largest EV battery maker launched six innovations at its Super Technology Day in Beijing, widening its lead over rivals.

top.title: Trump's White House ballroom contract reveals anonymous donor protections
top.description: The agreement, released after a court-ordered FOIA disclosure, exempts the White House from conflict of interest reviews governing the $400 million project.

top.title: Fifth Circuit upholds Texas Ten Commandments classroom law
top.description: The 9-8 en banc ruling reverses a lower court injunction, and civil rights groups say they will ask the Supreme Court to intervene.

top.title: Duffy marks one year of air traffic control overhaul, seeks $20B more from Congress
top.description: The Transportation secretary said the modernization is mostly on track but told lawmakers the FAA needs billions beyond the $12.5 billion already approved.

top.title: Trump's economic approval drops to 30% as Iran war fuels inflation
top.description: An AP-NORC poll found only 33% of adults approve of his overall job performance, with Republican support also declining sharply.

top.title: Americans killed in Mexico crash were CIA officers
top.description: The revelation has deepened a diplomatic rift, with President Sheinbaum ordering a probe into whether the operation violated Mexico's sovereignty laws.

top.title: Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick resigns ahead of expulsion vote
top.description: The Florida Democrat quit Congress Tuesday just before an Ethics Committee hearing expected to recommend her removal over alleged theft of $5 million in pandemic relief funds.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump permitting rules targeting wind and solar projects
top.description: A federal judge in Boston issued a preliminary injunction after finding the administration singled out renewable developers without clear rationale.

top.title: Macron pledges support for Lebanon ahead of Israel talks
top.description: France's president met Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam in Paris to bolster Beirut's position before Thursday's U.S.-hosted negotiations with Israel.

top.title: NATO revives Cold War-era sub hunting as Russia pushes into Arctic
top.description: Bloomberg reports that Russia's upgraded nuclear submarines are forcing NATO to overhaul anti-submarine warfare across the Arctic and North Atlantic.

top.title: Warsh assures senators Trump won’t dictate Fed policy on rates
top.description: Trump's Fed chair nominee testified he was never asked to commit to rate cuts, but a GOP senator's hold over the Powell probe clouds his path.

top.title: Dutch intelligence warns Chinese cyberattacks on West will intensify
top.description: The MIVD's 2025 annual report called on Europe to take greater responsibility for its own security as the rules-based international order erodes.

top.title: UNESCO report finds its sites sheltering 60% of mapped species
top.description: The first comprehensive assessment of all 2,260-plus UNESCO-designated sites found wildlife populations there held steady despite a 73% global decline.

top.title: FBI, House Oversight launch parallel probes into missing and dead scientists
top.description: At least 11 researchers linked to nuclear, aerospace, and defense work have died or vanished since 2022, prompting dual federal and congressional investigations.

top.title: Trump praises 'very good talks' with Anthropic after months-long feud
top.description: The president's remarks Tuesday mark a sharp reversal from ordering agencies to cut ties with the AI company in February.

top.title: Feds cut water releases amid historic Western drought
top.description: Colorado River system reservoirs hold just 36% of capacity as record-low snowpack and extreme heat compound the crisis.

top.title: Curiosity rover detects 'origin-of-life' molecules on Mars
top.description: A first-of-its-kind chemical experiment on the Red Planet found over 20 organic compounds, including a possible DNA precursor never before seen on Mars.

top.title: Trump's Board of Peace in talks with DP World on Gaza rebuild
top.description: A draft proposal envisions a "port-centric economic ecosystem" and supply chain system, as part of US efforts to privatise Gaza's reconstruction.

top.title: Japan scraps postwar ban on lethal weapons exports
top.description: Prime Minister Takaichi's Cabinet approved the sale of fighter jets, missiles, and warships to 17 partner nations.

top.title: Bezos's AI lab nears $10B round at $38B valuation
top.description: Project Prometheus, focused on physical AI for manufacturing and engineering, counts JPMorgan and BlackRock among its backers, according to the Financial Times.

top.title: Trump, GOP stockpile $1 billion for midterms amid shifting polls
top.description: New filings show Republicans hold roughly $1 billion in campaign cash, but the Cook Political Report has shifted four Senate races toward Democrats.

top.title: Over 4,200 US flights delayed as aviation crisis hits day 20
top.description: Severe spring weather drove 4,231 delays and 79 cancellations Monday, extending a post-Easter disruption streak with no recovery in sight.

top.title: Senate Democrats demand Hegseth explain civilian deaths in Iran
top.description: Eleven senators set a May 4 deadline for the defense secretary to justify cuts to Pentagon programs designed to protect noncombatants.

top.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to fund energy projects amid Iran war
top.description: Five presidential determinations target coal, LNG, petroleum and grid infrastructure as gas prices hover near $4 a gallon.

top.title: Iraq, Syria reopen Rabia border crossing after 13 years
top.description: The reopening restores all official border crossings between the two countries for the first time since civil war and ISIS disrupted the region.

top.title: Apple names John Ternus CEO as Tim Cook shifts to chairman role
top.description: Cook, who led Apple since 2011, will become executive chairman on Sept. 1 after a unanimous board vote.

top.title: Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer resigns amid misconduct probe
top.description: She is the third Cabinet secretary to leave Trump's second term, following months of inspector general scrutiny over alleged personal misconduct.

top.title: Vaccine-hesitant parents shift course as measles surges across the Americas
top.description: PAHO reports over 15,000 confirmed cases in the first 14 weeks of 2026, with two-thirds of patients unvaccinated.

top.title: Singer D4vd charged with first-degree murder of teen found in his Tesla
top.description: LA County DA Nathan Hochman announced charges Monday against the 21-year-old artist in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

top.title: Starmer admits misleading Parliament over Mandelson vetting scandal
top.description: The prime minister told MPs the error was inadvertent, but all major opposition parties have called for his resignation.

top.title: FBI Director Patel sues The Atlantic for $250M over report on drinking
top.description: The defamation lawsuit, filed Monday in D.C. federal court, alleges the magazine published false claims about his conduct with "actual malice."

top.title: UK pushes for toll-free Hormuz as ceasefire nears expiry
top.description: Cooper warns that the wrong precedent on navigation freedom in the strait would be "deeply damaging" for global economy and security.

top.title: CBP launches portal to refund $166B in tariffs struck down by Supreme Court
top.description: The CAPE system went live Monday at 8 a.m. ET, letting importers file claims in what could be the largest tariff repayment in U.S. history.

top.title: Supreme Court to hear Catholic preschools' challenge to Colorado LGBTQ nondiscrimination rules
top.description: The case tests whether religious schools can receive public funding while declining to enroll children from same-sex families.

top.title: Expert says Trump's Iran nuclear claim is 'just not true'
top.description: Harvard nuclear adviser told 60 Minutes that Iran still has enough enriched uranium for 10 to 11 bombs despite U.S. and Israeli strikes.

top.title: UAE warns it may settle oil trades in yuan without US dollar backstop
top.description: Abu Dhabi told Washington the Iran war has strained its reserves, and without a Fed swap line, it could pivot energy sales to Chinese currency.

top.title: Eni announces giant gas discovery offshore Indonesia
top.description: The Geliga-1 well in the Kutei Basin holds an estimated 5 trillion cubic feet of gas and 300 million barrels of condensate.

top.title: Samsung SDI signs first EV battery deal with Mercedes-Benz
top.description: The multi-year agreement will supply high-nickel batteries for Mercedes-Benz's next-generation compact and mid-size electric SUVs and coupes.

top.title: UniCredit unveils plan to transform Commerzbank
top.description: CEO Andrea Orcel is presenting a "value creation" strategy for the German lender, arguing it is too focused on short-term results.

top.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3, raising toll to 181
top.description: Sunday's Caribbean strike is the latest in Operation Southern Spear, a monthslong campaign that legal experts say violates international law.

top.title: Hegseth berated Army secretary in early clash, WSJ reports
top.description: A Wall Street Journal report reveals the defense secretary shouted at Dan Driscoll to "stay in his lane" soon after Driscoll's first day at the Pentagon.

top.title: Avalanche top Kings 2-1 in Stanley Cup playoff opener
top.description: Scott Wedgewood stopped 24 shots in his playoff debut as Colorado took Game 1 of the Western Conference First Round on Sunday.

top.title: Over 200 immigration judges replaced with 'deportation judges'
top.description: Former judges tell CBS News the unprecedented overhaul dismantles due process, as asylum approval rates fall to historic lows.

top.title: Israel building permanent military bases in Gaza, satellite images show
top.description: An Al Jazeera investigation found Israeli forces are expanding fortified outposts across Gaza as civilian reconstruction stalls and ceasefire violations mount.

top.title: FBI's Patel vows imminent arrests over 2020 election
top.description: Michigan officials issued a joint statement Sunday rejecting DOJ demands for 2024 ballots, calling the effort election interference.

top.title: Blue Origin reuses New Glenn booster but puts satellite in wrong orbit
top.description: The NG-3 mission marked New Glenn's first booster reuse, but an upper stage issue left AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite off course.

top.title: Hamas signals readiness to hand over some weapons in Gaza
top.description: Two officials told The New York Times the group would surrender police and security weapons, but its military wing's arsenal remains untouched.

top.title: Syrian billionaires invoked Trump name to help kill Caesar Act sanctions, NYT reports
top.description: The Al-Khayyat family lobbied Washington while securing over $12 billion in Syrian reconstruction contracts, according to the Times.

top.title: 8 children killed in mass shooting in Shreveport, La.
top.description: Police say 10 people were shot in a domestic disturbance that spanned three locations; victims ranged in age from 1 to 14.

top.title: Iran regains 60% of missile launchers during ceasefire, US intelligence estimates
top.description: Tehran has excavated about 100 additional launchers from bombed sites since the April 8 ceasefire, according to a New York Times report.

top.title: FBI, DOJ rush to rebuild after losing thousands of staff
top.description: The agencies are relaxing hiring standards and fast-tracking recruitment after mass departures during the Trump administration, according to the AP.

top.title: Vance strikes conciliatory tone with Pope Leo XIV after week of White House-Vatican tensions
top.description: The vice president wrote on X that he was "grateful" to the pope, days after warning him to "be careful" on theology.

top.title: Iran calls free transit through Hormuz 'a fiction' as Europe plans naval mission
top.description: Tehran asserted its right to control the strait, a day after 40-plus nations met in Paris to plan a multinational force to restore shipping access.

top.title: French peacekeeper killed as Lebanon ceasefire frays
top.description: A French UN soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday as Israel continued shelling despite Trump declaring strikes "prohibited."

top.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile into East Sea
top.description: South Korea's JCS detected at least one missile launched eastward on Sunday, the sixth known ballistic missile test by Pyongyang this year.

top.title: Penn State develops 3D-printed brain sensors tailored to each patient
top.description: Hydrogel electrodes shaped from MRI scans matched individual brain folds, improving signal quality without immune response in rat tests.

top.title: Trump Organization plans 70-story tower in Tbilisi, Georgia
top.description: The branded skyscraper would be the tallest building in the Georgian capital, marking the family firm's latest foreign deal during Trump's second term.

top.title: NATO chief Rutte calls US withdrawal speculation 'absurd'
top.description: Rutte told a German newspaper the American nuclear umbrella will remain Europe's "ultimate guarantor of security."

top.title: Iranians fear regime will tighten grip once ceasefire holds
top.description: As a fragile truce pauses U.S.-Israeli strikes, Iranians told Reuters they dread the regime redirecting its full coercive apparatus inward.

top.title: Trump backs 7 challengers against Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
top.description: Indiana's May 5 primary tests whether Trump can punish state lawmakers who defied his push to redraw congressional maps.

top.title: Egypt, Pakistan building framework for lasting US-Iran peace
top.description: Four regional powers are intensifying diplomacy at the Antalya forum as a fragile ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

top.title: Australia, Japan sign $7B stealth frigate deal
top.description: The A$10 billion contract for 11 Mogami-class warships is Japan's biggest defense export since lifting its postwar military export ban.

top.title: Trump signs bill extending spy powers until April 30
top.description: The 10-day stopgap followed a chaotic overnight House session where conservative Republicans blocked longer renewals of warrantless surveillance authority.

top.title: Tornado outbreak batters Midwest as severe storms continue
top.description: At least 10 tornadoes were reported Friday as storms unleashed massive hail and destructive winds from Texas to the Great Lakes.

top.title: Israeli fire kills two UNICEF water truck drivers in Gaza
top.description: UNICEF suspended operations at northern Gaza's only truck filling point for the water supply line serving Gaza City after the attack.

top.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani meets with Obama amid feud with Trump
top.description: The sit-down at a Bronx child-care center caps months of rapport-building between the democratic socialist mayor and the former president.

top.title: Alito not expected to retire, sources say, cooling Supreme Court vacancy talk
top.description: The 76-year-old justice has hired clerks for next term, ending weeks of speculation about a Trump appointment opportunity.

top.title: Pakistan deployed fighter jets to escort Iran delegation home from talks
top.description: About two dozen jets escorted Iranian negotiators to Tehran after officials raised concerns about a possible Israeli strike on their aircraft.

top.title: Trump reverses course, renews Russian oil sanctions waiver
top.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent said just two days earlier that the waiver would not be renewed. The new license runs through May 16.

top.title: UNICEF reports 172 children killed as Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
top.description: The U.S.-brokered 10-day truce began at midnight local time on April 17 after 46 days of fighting that killed over 2,100 people.

top.title: RFK Jr. calls Danish Tylenol-autism study 'garbage' at House hearing
top.description: Kennedy dismissed research on 1.5 million children finding no link between prenatal acetaminophen use and autism, demanding its retraction.

top.title: Trump signs order to fast-track psychedelic drug reviews
top.description: The executive order directs the FDA to expedite approval of MDMA, psilocybin, and other psychedelics and allocates $50 million for ibogaine research.

top.title: Breeding top 1-5% of corals could boost reef survival, study finds
top.description: Newcastle University-led research published in Current Biology shows assisted evolution can help corals endure heatwaves, but only with aggressive, multigenerational selection.

top.title: Xanadu CEO becomes billionaire as Nvidia quantum AI push lifts stock
top.description: Christian Weedbrook's 51.8% stake in the photonic quantum computing firm is now worth over $1.6 billion, three weeks after its IPO.

top.title: Appeals court lets Trump resume White House ballroom work
top.description: A three-judge panel temporarily lifted a lower court injunction, allowing the $400 million project to continue ahead of a June 5 hearing.

top.title: FAA receives 6,000 air traffic controller applications in 12 hours
top.description: The gamer-targeted recruitment campaign drew a flood of interest as the agency, roughly 3,000 controllers short, nears its 8,000-application cap.

top.title: Data center industry scrambles as backlash threatens trillions in projects
top.description: Industry executives worry a coordinated response is lacking as communities block projects and states reconsider billions in tax breaks.

top.title: Israeli drone kills one in southern Lebanon hours into ceasefire
top.description: Trump declared Israel "PROHIBITED" from bombing Lebanon, but an Israeli drone struck a motorcyclist in the south on the first day of the truce.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV draws massive crowd at Douala Mass
top.description: The pope challenged corruption and urged youth to reject violence during the largest gathering of his 11-day African tour.

top.title: Researchers produce hydrogen from alcohol using iron and UV light
top.description: A control experiment that was expected to fail led to a simple method rivaling the performance of expensive catalysts.

top.title: Iran's buried Pickaxe Mountain site looms over nuclear talks
top.description: With an April 22 ceasefire deadline days away, analysts are pressing Trump to neutralize a deeply buried facility that U.S. bombs cannot reach.

top.title: Newsom's PAC spent $1.5M buying his memoir for donors
top.description: New federal filings show about 67,000 supporters received copies, accounting for roughly two-thirds of the book's total print sales.

top.title: Wright tells Congress AI data centers are straining the grid
top.description: The Energy secretary warned that surging electricity demand from AI and reshoring is putting historic pressure on U.S. power infrastructure.

top.title: Trump seeks 90-day pause in $10B IRS suit amid talks
top.description: Lawyers for the president filed a motion Friday to halt the case while both sides negotiate a potential settlement with the Justice Department's consent.
